questions.question: Can I replant new trees to replace those removed, in lieu of a payment? Am I allowed to plant non-native species as replacements?
questions.answer: Yes, you can replant new trees to replace those removed, as the Village Engineer has the authority to require replacement trees of comparable size or species to be planted as a condition of granting a tree removal permit (§ 281-9 B). However, the replacement trees must be native to the Northeast United States, excluding invasive species, unless specifically approved as part of a tree replacement plan (§ 281-3, Replacement Tree definition). Therefore, planting non-native species as replacements is not generally allowed unless specifically approved.
questions.gap: Consider adding specific guidelines for monitoring the survival of replacement trees over a defined period to ensure successful establishment and compliance with the replacement requirements.
